section.heading: Consultation regarding oil and gas leasing on public land

subsection.heading: In general
subsection.chapeau: Not later than 180 days after August 8, 2005, the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture shall enter into a memorandum of understanding regarding oil and gas leasing on—

paragraph.content: public land under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of the Interior; and
National Forest System land under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of Agriculture.

subsection.heading: Contents
subsection.chapeau: The memorandum of understanding shall include provisions that—

paragraph.chapeau: establish administrative procedures and lines of authority that ensure timely processing of—

subparagraph.content: surface use plans of operation, including steps for processing surface use plans; and
applications for permits to drill consistent with applicable timelines;

paragraph.content: eliminate duplication of effort by providing for coordination of planning and environmental compliance efforts;

subparagraph.content: only as restrictive as necessary to protect the resource for which the stipulations are applied;

paragraph.chapeau: establish a joint data retrieval system that is capable of—

subparagraph.content: tracking applications and formal requests made in accordance with procedures of the Federal onshore oil and gas leasing program; and
providing information regarding the status of the applications and requests within the Department of the Interior and the Department of Agriculture; and

paragraph.chapeau: establish a joint geographic information system mapping system for use in—

subparagraph.content: tracking surface resource values to aid in resource management; and
processing surface use plans of operation and applications for permits to drill.
